summary: A high‑energy 1978 show with stellar Deal, Dupree’s and Around & Around performances captured on a solid audience tape.
review: The concert shines thanks to Jerry’s inspired guitar work (especially on Deal and Dupree’s), Keith Godchaux’s lush keyboards and a thunderous drum section.  Although the AUD source isn’t pristine – it runs a touch fast, mids are recessed and occasional mudiness appears – the tape is consistently listenable and lets every instrument be heard.  Fans repeatedly cite the soaring Black Peter, the marathon Samson & Delilah and the electrifying Sat Night as highlights.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical performance praised across most reviews (70% weight) with a consistently decent but not flawless audience recording (30% weight).

recording_quality.technical_notes: Not top‑notch: runs slightly fast, mids need a boost and occasional mud, but overall clear enough to hear each player.  Many reviewers note it is “listenable” and among the better audience tapes.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows with high energy; several reviewers mention songs segued without full endings, creating a seamless, almost nonstop rock momentum despite the slightly brisk tempo.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for inspired playing, great tone on Deal and Dupree's, and an improving vocal performance.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is highlighted as strong; some reviewers suggest boosting the mids to let his lines shine.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux’s keyboards are singled out as especially good; Donna Jean’s vocals also receive commendation.
band_member_comments.Drums: Post‑drum sections described as amazing, with drummers “ON” throughout.
